description: Create 'What You're Missing' Pro Analytics Preview section for the Dashboard, and add Pro feature flags to the Dashboard page. Shows free users a tantalizing preview of analytics insights (spending patterns, savings goals, category breakdown) they would get with Pro, using blurred/locked sections that trigger the upgrade modal.
steps: Create components/premium/ProAnalyticsPreview.tsx - A dashboard section that shows a preview of Pro analytics features. Include 3-4 mock insight cards: 'Monthly Spending Trends' (with a blurred mini-chart using Recharts), 'Category Breakdown' (blurred pie chart), 'Savings Goal Tracker' (blurred progress bars), 'AI-Powered Insights' (blurred text insights). Each card uses ProFeatureWrapper from FEAT-001 with featureName='Advanced Analytics'. Style with Card components and the existing emerald color scheme.

Create components/premium/ProInsightTeaser.tsx - A smaller teaser widget showing one specific insight like 'Based on your 12 calculations, Pro users save an average of 23% more' with a blurred details section and upgrade CTA. This uses realistic-looking but static mock data to create FOMO.

Update app/dashboard/page.tsx to include the ProAnalyticsPreview component below the existing DashboardHistory section. Import and render it for all users (the component internally handles Pro vs Free display via ProFeatureWrapper).
Add a 'Pro Features' section heading between DashboardHistory and ProAnalyticsPreview with text like 'Unlock Advanced Insights' and a subheading explaining what Pro analytics provides.
acceptance_criteria: ProAnalyticsPreview renders 3-4 blurred insight cards for free users with Pro badges
Each blurred card opens UpgradeGateModal when clicked by free users
Pro users would see the cards without blur (though actual analytics data is mock/static for now)
ProInsightTeaser shows a compelling single-stat teaser with upgrade CTA
Dashboard page includes the new ProAnalyticsPreview section
verification: Run npx next build from /projects/sandbox/cost-smart and confirm no errors
Verify TypeScript types are correct by checking build output for type errors
